title: Properties of Sample Mean
body: A team of researchers is studying the football game attendance of UC Berkeley students. In the study, the team collects a simple random sample of $$100$$ students from all UC Berkeley students. The team collects the following data for each student: • Xi : The number of home football games attended by student i, for $$i=1$$, $$2$$, . . . , $$100$$. For the purposes of this question, you can assume that the Xi’s are independent and identically distributed. The researchers use $$X_{1...X,100}$$ to estimate properties of the distribution of the number of home football games attended by all UC Berkeley students. a) We define an estimator \alpha_hat, which is the sample mean: \alpha_hat=(1/100)*sum{i\=1}{100}{X_i}. Let $$\alpha=E[X_i]$$ be the true average number of home games attended by UC Berkeley students.
